subject: [PATCH v4] mm: mglru: fix stale batch updates after memcg reparenting

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the rcu_read_unlock() call is misplaced and suggested moving it next to other lruvec_lock() primitives for better code organization, citing ease of understanding and consistency with the rest of the API.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, that tripped me up too. And it makes me think Shakeel was right
after all: this should live next to the other lruvec_lock() primitives.

Sure, MGLRU is the only user, but it's still much easier to understand
this if the code sits next to the rest of the API (and the unlock!).

lruvec_live_lock_irq()?

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch fixes an issue where batch updates in the Memory Global LRU (MGLRU) mechanism become stale after a memory cgroup is reparented, leading to incorrect accounting and potential performance issues.
